idea-plugin.name: TypeGhost
idea-plugin.description:
A teleprompter for your code. Write it once, "type" it live.

TypeGhost replays pre-written code in your editor with human-like typing — irregular rhythm, thinking pauses, even typos that get noticed and fixed. Perfect for conference talks, screencasts, tutorials and classroom demos: no more typos in front of 300 people.

Two ways to fake it

  • Auto mode — the code types itself at a human pace while you narrate, stopping at checkpoints wherever you want to talk.
  • Hacker mode — you mash any keys, the real script comes out. You physically cannot make a typo.

Script directives

Comments with a ~ glued to the comment marker control playback and are stripped from the output: //~ pause 800, //~ checkpoint intro. Works in any language (#~, --~, ;;~, %~).

Find the commands under Tools → TypeGhost. Whatever the simulation does, the final buffer is byte-identical to your script — your demo cannot derail.

Works in IntelliJ IDEA, PhpStorm, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, Rider and every other IntelliJ-based IDE. Sources, web demo and VS Code extension on GitHub.
idea-plugin.change-notes: 0.1.0 — first release: auto mode with human-like typing and checkpoints, hacker mode, pause/resume, script directives.

action.description: Auto-type the clipboard content at the caret
Auto-type a file's content at the caret
Your keystrokes type the clipboard content
Your keystrokes type a file's content
Pause or resume playback; also continues past checkpoints
Stop TypeGhost and hand the keyboard back
